heading: Defense of lack of mental responsibility
label: 949k Defense of lack of mental responsibility
notes: Prior ProvisionsA prior section 949k, added , , , related to the defense of lack of mental responsibility, prior to the general amendment of this chapter by .Pub. L. 109–366, § 3(a)(1)120 Stat. 2615Pub. L. 111–84Oct. 17, 2006
amendments: Prior ProvisionsA prior section 949k, added , , , related to the defense of lack of mental responsibility, prior to the general amendment of this chapter by .Pub. L. 109–366, § 3(a)(1)120 Stat. 2615Pub. L. 111–84Oct. 17, 2006

children.heading: .—Affirmative Defense
children.label: a .—Affirmative Defense
children.text: It is an affirmative defense in a trial by military commission under this chapter that, at the time of the commission of the acts constituting the offense, the accused, as a result of a severe mental disease or defect, was unable to appreciate the nature and quality or the wrongfulness of the acts. Mental disease or defect does not otherwise constitute a defense.

children.heading: .—Burden of Proof
children.label: b .—Burden of Proof
children.text: The accused in a military commission under this chapter has the burden of proving the defense of lack of mental responsibility by clear and convincing evidence.
subject to subsection (d), not guilty by reason of lack of mental responsibility.

children.heading: .—Majority Vote Required for Finding
children.label: d .—Majority Vote Required for Finding
children.text: The accused shall be found not guilty by reason of lack of mental responsibility under subsection (c)(3) only if a majority of the members present at the time the vote is taken determines that the defense of lack of mental responsibility has been established.
